expectations.text: Shows complete YAML config with correct top-level sections (infisical, auth, sinks, templates)
expectations.evidence: Agent config has top-level sections: infisical, auth, sinks, templates with proper YAML structure

expectations.text: Uses listSecrets or listSecretsByProjectSlug template function with correct 4-arg signature
expectations.evidence: Uses 'listSecretsByProjectSlug "my-app" "production" "/"' with correct 3-arg signature. Also mentions 'Optional 4th param: {"recursive": true, "expandSecretReferences": true}' for optional 4th parameter

expectations.text: Uses correct Go template syntax ({{- with ... }} / {{- range . }} / {{ .Key }}={{ .Value }})
expectations.evidence: Template shows '{{- with listSecretsByProjectSlug...', '{{- range . }}', '{{ .Key }}={{ .Value }}' with proper whitespace control

expectations.text: Includes polling-interval configuration for auto-update
expectations.evidence: Template config shows 'polling-interval: "5m"'

expectations.text: Includes destination-path pointing to a .env file
expectations.evidence: Template shows 'destination-path: /infisical/secrets/.env'

expectations.text: Mentions execute.command option for triggering app restart on change
expectations.evidence: Shows 'execute: command: "kill -HUP 1"' to signal Node process to reload

expectations.text: Uses aws-iam auth type (not universal-auth with client-id/secret)
expectations.evidence: Auth config shows 'auth: type: "aws-iam"' with 'identity-id' field, not universal-auth

expectations.text: Shows correct auth config with identity-id field
expectations.evidence: Config shows 'identity-id: "<your-machine-identity-id>"' with comment 'Paste the ID from Step 1'

expectations.text: Shows ECS task definition pattern with shared volume between agent and app containers
expectations.evidence: Complete ECS task definition with two containers (infisical-agent and app) sharing 'secrets' volume with mountPoints specified for both

expectations.text: Uses correct template function (listSecrets or listSecretsByProjectSlug) with proper signature
expectations.evidence: Template uses 'listSecretsByProjectSlug "my-project" "prod" "/"' with proper 3-arg signature

expectations.text: Mentions exit-after-auth option for init-style sidecar pattern
expectations.evidence: Config shows 'exit-after-auth: true' with comment 'Render once and exit' and separate section 'Optional: Continuous Secret Updates' that shows setting to false

expectations.text: Does not require manually managing any credentials or access keys
expectations.evidence: Uses AWS IAM authentication through ECS task role - no explicit credential files or keys needed to be managed

expectations.text: Template uses listSecrets or listSecretsByProjectSlug for static secrets
expectations.evidence: Template 1 uses '{{- with listSecretsByProjectSlug "my-project" "prod" "/" }}' for static secrets

expectations.text: Template uses dynamicSecret function for PostgreSQL credentials
expectations.evidence: Template 2 uses '{{ with dynamicSecret "my-project" "prod" "/" "postgres-creds" "1h" }}' for PostgreSQL credentials

expectations.text: dynamicSecret function has correct 5-arg signature (project-slug, env, path, name, ttl)
expectations.evidence: dynamicSecret call shows all 5 args: project-slug (my-project), env (prod), path (/), name (postgres-creds), ttl (1h)

expectations.text: Shows .DB_USERNAME and .DB_PASSWORD fields from dynamic secret output
expectations.evidence: Template shows 'DB_USER={{ .DB_USERNAME }}' and 'DB_PASSWORD={{ .DB_PASSWORD }}'

expectations.text: Mentions auto-renewal behavior (agent renews before expiration)
expectations.evidence: "Auto-renewal: Before TTL expires (typically at 80% of TTL), agent auto-renews the lease" and detailed lease lifecycle explanation

expectations.text: Recommends revoke-credentials-on-shutdown: true for cleanup
expectations.evidence: Top-level config shows 'revoke-credentials-on-shutdown: true' with explanation in best practices
